name: Hoppin John
recipeIngredient: 1 large onion, peeled and finely chopped
recipeInstructions:
1. Soak the black-eyed peas overnight in water that covers them by at least 2 inches.
2. Drain the soaked peas and set aside.
3. In a large Dutch oven, heat the oil over medium heat. Add the onion and sauté until it becomes translucent.
4. Add the peas, garlic salt, and oregano to the onion mixture. Stir to combine.
5. Pour in the chicken stock and bring the mixture to a simmer.
6. Cover and cook slowly for about 30-40 minutes, or until the peas are tender.
7. If making ahead of time, cover and refrigerate. To reheat later, simply warm the peas before serving.
8. For each serving, place half a cup of cooked peas over half a cup of rice.
